layman_headline: Crypto markets are in deep fear mode — patience is the best trade right now.
layman_explanation: Bitcoin has dropped below $60,000 and is on track for rare back-to-back quarterly losses. The entire market is in 'Extreme Fear' at 12 out of 100, and it's getting worse day by day. Most altcoins are bleeding heavily. While extreme fear can eventually create buying opportunities, there's no clear sign yet that selling has stopped — so jumping in now is like trying to catch a falling knife.
layman_action: Stay in cash and wait. The market is still falling and there's no confirmed reversal yet. Watch for Bitcoin to stabilize above $60,000 and fear to start easing before committing capital.
expert_reasoning: Every swing candidate has significant flaws this cycle. VELVET is up 292% in a week with RSI at 70 — classic blowoff risk in a fearful market, terrible risk/reward. GWEI is similarly overextended at +51% weekly. KITE has a -2.0 chart bias with Three Black Crows — actively crashing. WLD dropped 31% but chart bias is bearish with a Bearish Marubozu. PEPE has a -1.4 chart bias in confirmed downtrend. SOL shows the best relative strength among large caps (+2.49% daily while most are red) with RSI at 42 approaching oversold, but its chart bias is still -0.7 with price below all major MAs and a doji signaling indecision — not yet a confirmed entry. The Fear & Greed index at 12 and worsening suggests capitulation may not be complete. BTC dominance at 55.6% means capital is fleeing alts into Bitcoin, making alt swing trades especially risky. The prudent move is to wait for a reversal confirmation — a hammer or morning star on SOL near $66 support would be actionable.
risk_factors: BTC below $60K with potential for further capitulation toward $55K, which would drag all alts down significantly
Fear & Greed at 12 and still worsening — no sign of sentiment bottoming yet
BTC dominance at 55.6% means capital continues rotating out of alts into Bitcoin
watch_list: Bitcoin price action around $58,000-$60,000 — a decisive close below $58K would signal deeper correction
SOL holding $66 support level — a bounce there with volume would create a proper entry
Fear & Greed index — any reversal from 12 back above 20 would signal early sentiment recovery
market_context: Bitcoin is below $60,000 heading for rare back-to-back quarterly losses, with Fear & Greed at an extreme 12/100 and worsening. This is a defensive environment where capital preservation beats aggressive entries.
context_banner: Extreme Fear dominates crypto markets with BTC below $60K — no confirmed reversal signals yet, patience is the highest-conviction play.

key_signals.description: Fear & Greed index at 12 (Extreme Fear) and trending lower from 18 yesterday — capitulation may still be underway, making new entries premature.
Bitcoin fell below $60,000 and is on track for rare back-to-back quarterly losses, signaling a sustained bearish macro environment.
Bitcoin's biggest ETF is becoming a 'sell wall bulls have to break' — institutional flows are turning into overhead resistance.
SOL RSI at 42 approaching oversold while showing best 7-day relative performance (-1.4%) among top 20 — first coin to watch for reversal entry.
Bullish Bitcoin RSI divergence has analysts calling for a 2022-style bear market bottom — potential bottom forming but not yet confirmed.
